task_completion.description: The agent opens the sales spreadsheet containing the date data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open, displaying the sales data with a column for dates.

task_completion.description: The agent selects or interacts with the date column to apply formatting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The column containing the dates is highlighted, or a format cells dialog is open for the date cells.

task_completion.description: Dates originally in MM/DD/YYYY format are converted to YYYY-MM-DD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first few date entries (originally like 01/05/2024) are visible in the spreadsheet as 2024-01-05, 2024-01-06, etc.

task_completion.description: Dates originally in DD-MM-YYYY format are converted to YYYY-MM-DD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The middle date entries (originally like 08-01-2024) are visible in the spreadsheet as 2024-01-08, 2024-01-09, etc.

task_completion.description: All dates in the spreadsheet are consistently in the YYYY-MM-DD format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Scanning the visible rows in the date column shows only the YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., 2024-02-01, 2024-03-15) with no other formats present.

integrity.description: The agent used the spreadsheet GUI to format the dates.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a spreadsheet application's menus, dialogs, or cells, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual spreadsheet data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formatting changes are visible within the spreadsheet grid itself, not just in a separate text file or terminal output.

integrity.description: The agent used formatting tools or formulas rather than manually typing out every single date.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of using 'Format Cells', 'Find and Replace', or formulas, rather than individually editing each of the 20 cells one by one.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spreadsheet contains 20 rows of data with dates spanning Jan 5 to Mar 15, 2024 in chronological order. The original dates have three formats: MM/DD/YYYY (e.g., 01/05/2024 for Jan 5), DD-MM-YYYY (e.g., 08-01-2024 for Jan 8), and YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2024-02-01). The task requires converting all of them to YYYY-MM-DD format. Specifically, verify that 01/05/2024 becomes 2024-01-05, and 08-01-2024 becomes 2024-01-08. All dates must remain in strict chronological order.
